author: Tertullian
book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
Our Flesh in the Resurrection Capable, Without Losing Its Essential Identity, of Bearing the Changed Conditions of Eternal Life, or of Death Eternal.

ccel.title: ANF03. Latin Christianity: Its Founder, Tertullian

source.title: ANF03. Latin Christianity: Its Founder, Tertullian

title: Our Flesh in the Resurrection Capable, Without Losing Its Essential Identity, of Bearing the Changed Conditions of Eternal Life, or of Death Eternal.

source.title: ANF03. Latin Christianity: Its Founder, Tertullian

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: But, you object, the world to come bears the character of a different dispensation, even an eternal one; and therefore, you maintain, that the non-eternal substance of this life is incapable of possessing a state of such different features.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: This would be true enough, if man were made for the future dispensation, and not the dispensation for man.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: The apostle, however, in his epistle says, “Whether it be the world, or life, or death, or things present, or things to come; all are yours:” and he here constitutes us heirs even of the future world.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: Isaiah gives you no help when he says, “All flesh is grass;” and in another passage, “All flesh shall see the salvation of God.”

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: It is the issues of men, not their substances, which he distinguishes.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: But who does not hold that the judgment of God consists in the twofold sentence, of salvation and of punishment?

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: Therefore it is that “all flesh is grass,” which is destined to the fire; and “all flesh shall see the salvation of God,” which is ordained to eternal life.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: For myself, I am quite sure that it is in no other flesh than my own that I have committed adultery, nor in any other flesh am I striving after continence.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: If there be any one who bears about in his person two instruments of lasciviousness, he has it in his power, to be sure, to mow down “the grass” of the unclean flesh, and to reserve for himself only that which shall see the salvation of God.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: But when the same prophet represents to us even nations sometimes estimated as “the small dust of the balance,” and as “less than nothing, and vanity,” and sometimes as about to hope and “trust in the name” and arm of the Lord, are we at all misled respecting the Gentile nations by the diversity of statement?

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: Are some of them to turn believers, and are others accounted dust, from any difference of nature?

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: Nay, rather Christ has shone as the true light on the nations within the ocean’s limits, and from the heaven which is over us all.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: Why, it is even on this earth that the Valentinians have gone to school for their errors; and there will be no difference of condition, as respects their body and soul, between the nations which believe and those which do not believe.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: Precisely, then, as He has put a distinction of state, not of nature, amongst the same nations, so also has He discriminated their flesh, which is one and the same substance in those nations, not according to their material structure, but according to the recompense of their merit.
